hero.headline: Experiential Due Diligence at Asset Origin
hero.subheadline: On-site immersion trips across 20 alternative asset classes for institutional-grade evaluation
hero.body: Direct access to operating facilities, management teams, and local market dynamics across infrastructure, real assets, and emerging sectors. 40% of participants allocate within 90 days post-trip (vs. 12% desktop-only diligence).

sections.title: Asset-Class Coverage
sections.content:
Twenty immersion categories spanning traditional alternatives (real estate, agriculture, infrastructure) and emerging sectors (AI compute, vertical farming, battery storage). Each trip provides 48-72 hour on-site access to operating assets, financial records, and management Q&A.

Average group size: 8-12 participants. Historical allocation rate: 40% of attendees deploy capital within 90 days post-trip, compared to 12% for desktop-only due diligence processes (Source: ZILLIO Trip Analytics 2023-2025).

Trips operate under NDA protocols. Participants receive pre-trip data rooms (financials, operating metrics, market context) 14 days prior. Post-trip deliverables include facility audit reports, comparative analysis vs. peer assets, and allocation framework templates.

sections.title: Sector Distribution
sections.content:
Infrastructure: AI data centers (Northern Virginia, Frankfurt), battery storage facilities (California, South Australia), fiber optic networks (Southeast Asia). Real Assets: Dubai multi-family developments, Portuguese wine estates, Japanese timber forests, Norwegian aquaculture operations. Emerging: Vertical farms (Netherlands, Singapore), carbon capture plants (Iceland), modular nuclear reactors (Ontario).

Each category represents 30-90 minute direct facility access, 60-90 minute management presentation, and 120 minute participant-led Q&A. Historical data shows 73% of participants rank on-site inspection and management interaction as primary value drivers vs. 27% for market context briefings.

Sector selection criteria: minimum $500M global investable universe, demonstrated institutional participation (pension funds, endowments, family offices), transparent operating metrics, and regulatory clarity in target jurisdictions.

sections.title: Participant Outcomes
sections.content:
"Allocated $15M to Portuguese vineyard operator within 45 days of trip. On-site soil analysis and harvest observation validated desktop research that showed 8-12% net yields vs. peer average 5-7%." — Family Office CIO, $2.3B AUM, Singapore

"Dubai multi-family tour revealed occupancy trends (94% vs. reported 89%) and tenant quality (67% corporate vs. 48% individual) that shifted our MENA allocation from 8% to 14% of real estate portfolio." — Endowment Investment Committee Member, $800M AUM, United States

"Vertical farm trip in Netherlands demonstrated 40% lower energy costs vs. North American operators due to grid integration and waste heat recovery. Revised pro forma from 11% IRR to 16% IRR based on observed operating efficiency." — Single Family Office Principal, $450M AUM, Canada
metrics: 16% revised IRR vs. 11% initial pro forma

sections.title: 2026 Calendar
sections.content:
Q2: AI data centers (Northern Virginia, May 12-14), Dubai real estate (June 18-20), Norwegian aquaculture (June 25-27). Q3: Portuguese vineyards (September 8-10), Japanese timber forests (September 22-24), Netherlands vertical farms (October 6-8). Q4: Ontario modular nuclear (November 3-5), Iceland carbon capture (November 17-19), California battery storage (December 1-3).

Registration opens 90 days prior to trip date. Participant qualification: verified accredited investor status, minimum $10M liquid assets, signed NDA and code of conduct. Average cost per trip: $8,500-12,500 (includes facility access fees, logistics, accommodations, excludes flights).

Priority access: ZILLIO members receive 30-day early registration window and 15% fee reduction. Trip capacity: 8-12 participants (firm maximum to preserve access quality and management engagement depth).
